name: Reuben Casserole
directions: Put half of the rye bread cubes into a greased 15 x 10 Pyrex.
Top with the sauerkraut.
Mix the mayo and Russian dressing together and spoon half on top the sauerkraut.
Top with 1 cup of the Swiss cheese.
Top with the corned beef.
Top with the rest of the Mayo/dressing mix.
Lastly, top with the remainder of the bread cubes and the remainder of the Swiss cheese.
Drizzle the melted butter over all.
Cover with foil and bake at 350 degrees for 40 minutes.
Uncover and bake an additional 10 minutes.
Serve hot --  Enjoy!

name: Reuben Casserole
directions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
Spray with cooking spray or butter a 13 x 9" baking dish.
Spread sauerkraut evenly over bottom of prepared baking dish.
Cover sauerkraut with corned beef.
Spread Thousand Island dressing evenly over corned beef.
Arrange slices of Swiss cheese to cover dressing.
Cube the loaf of rye bread and toss with melted butter.
Sprinkle over top of casserole.
Bake, uncovered, 30 minutes at 350 degrees.

name: Reuben Bake
directions: Toss noodles with 2 tablespoons butter and spread in bottom of a greased 9 x 13-inch baking dish.
Add a layer of kraut, then meat.
Combine the salad dressing, catsup, grated onion and dill seed and spread over the meat.
Cover with cheese.
Toss bread crumbs in 2 tablespoons melted butter and sprinkle over dish.
Bake at 350 degrees for 35 minutes.

name: Low-carb Reuben Casserole
directions: In a large casserole dish sprayed with PAM, layer slices of corned beef (the thin sliced - not chipped - works best). Kind of scrunch them up with an "uneven approach" so it's not a slab of corned beef.

Drain saurkraut of 1/2 its juice and lay atop corned beef (if you like the sweeter variety of kraut, thoroughly rinse off canned/jarred juice and replace with a bit of cool water -- toss 1/2 tsp Brown Sugar Twin or Splenda into it before adding to the casserole dish.) Top with swiss cheese layer and bake at 350?F for 15 minutes or until cheese is melty but not liquidy.
While dish is baking, in separate bowl mix Thousand Island Dressing, Mayonnaise, and Relish.
Remove casserole dish from oven, slice in 4 serving-size squares and top with dollops of dressing mixture.

name: Jan's Reuben Casserole
directions: Line bottom of 9x13 pan with frozen peirogie.
Spread sauerkraut on top.
Add the corned beef.  Top with thousand island dressing.
Top all with provolone cheese.
Bake at 350 deg. for 45 minutes.

name: Reuben Casserole
directions: Preheat oven to 350.
Spray 9 x 13" pan with cooking spray.
Place corned beef strips along bottom of pan.
Spread sauerkraut over top evenly.
Pour 1000 Island Drssing evenly over top.
Layer slices of swiss cheese until evenly covered.
Brown/sautee torn pumpernickel bread with butter in fry pan until browned and crisp.
Layer bread over top of casserole.
Bake at 350 for 40 minutes.
Let cool 10 minutes, serve.

name: Reuben Casserole for 2
directions: Place the sauerkraut in an ungreased 3-cup baking dish.
Sprinkle with caraway seeds.
Layer with the tomato wedges, salad dressing, corned beef and Swiss cheese.
Toss the bread cubes and the butter and sprinkle cubes over the top.
Bake, uncovered, at 375 degrees for 25 to 30 minutes or until heated through.

name: Layered Reuben Corned Beef Casserole
directions: Preheat oven to 400 degrees.
Rinse and drain sauerkraut. Place in the bottom of a greased 1 1/2 quart casserole.
Mix together dressing and mayonnaise.
Top the sauerkraut with half the cheese, then half of the dressing, all of the corned beef, then the remaining cheese, and remaining dressing.
Bake until it starts to bubble.
Top with rye slices, brushed with melted butter and bake 10 minutes more.
You could also just bake it until bubbly and spoon it onto rye or pumpernickle bread instead of the party rye, if you wanted to.

name: Chicken Reuben Casserole
directions: Sprinkle chicken with salt and pepper; place in a lightly greased 13- x 9-inch baking dish.
Layer chicken evenly with sauerkraut, corned beef, and Swiss cheese. Pour dressing over top.
Combine breadcrumbs, butter, and parsley; sprinkle over casserole.
Bake casserole at 350 for 35 minutes or until golden and bubbly.

name: Reuben Casserole
directions: Preheat oven to 400°.
Spray 8x8 pan with non-stick cooking spray.
Layer bottom to top: sauerkraut, tomato slices, corned beef, top with cheese.
Bake uncovered for 25-30 minutes or until bubbly and cheese is melted.
Allow to cool 5 minutes before slicing into 4 servings.
Serving suggestions: Toast rye bread and smear the toast with Thousand Island dressing) then   top with rueben casserole
OR: Top the open face sandwich with Thousand Island dressing.
You don't have to toast the rye bread, but I sure like it that way.
NOTE: you can make a quick & easy Thousand Island dressing by mixing equal parts of mayonnaise with bottle cocktail or chili sauce.

name: Reuben  Casserole
directions: In a large bowl combine roast beef, sauerkraut, soup, cheese, milk, and dressing.
Pour mixture into an ungreased 1 1/2 quart casserole dish.
In a small bowl stir together bread crumbs and melted butter. Sprinkle over beef mixture.
Bake uncovered at 375°F for about 25 minutes.

name: Reuben Bake
directions: Heat oven to 350 degrees.
Coat 13x9x2-inch baking dish with nonstick cooking spray.
Cook noodles following package directions.
Drain and toss with 2 T of the butter.
Place in prepared dish; top with sauerkraut, then corned beef.
In a bowl, mix mayonnaise, ketchup, pickle and onion.
Spread over corned beef.
Top with tomato and cheese.
Pour milk evenly over the top.
Melt remaining 2 T butter and toss with rye cracker crumbs and caraway seeds; sprinkle over top.
Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes.
Let stand 10 minutes, then serve.

name: Reuben Casserole
directions: Preheat Oven to 375 degrees.
Combine and mix together sauerkraut, onion, parsley, and caraway seeds.
Top with 1 cup Swiss Cheese, 1/3 Cup of Dressing and meat.
Top with remaining cheese and dressing.  Sprinkle with prepared bread cubes, and bake near center of the oven for 35 to 40 minutes at 375 degrees.

name: Reuben Casserole
directions: preheat oven to 350 degrees F.
In a medium bowl combine the soup, milk, onion and mustard.  Mix well and set aside.

Spread sauerkrout into the bottom of a lightly greased 9 x 13 inch baking dish.  Top with uncooked noodles, then spoon soup mixture evenly over noodles.  Cover all with sausage and top with cheese.  Lastly, combine bread crumbs and melted butter in a small bowl and mix together; sprinkle mixture over cheese.
Cover baking dish tightly with aluminum foil and bake in oven for 1 hour or until noodles are tender.

name: Reuben Casserole
directions: Preheat oven to 350.
Coat bread pieces in the melted butter and set aside.
Break up the corned beef into a greased 9x13 baking dish.
Drizzle with thousand island dressing.
Cover with the sauerkraut and then sprinkle a layer of the cheese.
Top with tomatoes (you can leave these out, I think it adds a bit of color!).
Cover with breadcrumbs and bake in oven for 30 minutes or until bubbly.

name: Reuben Casserole
directions: Preheat oven to 400. Saute' the onion in 2 tablespoons butter until onion is limp. Add apple and saute' until soft.Add caraway seeds, kraut and corned beef and saute' 1 minute to blend flavors. Place mixture in medium casserole dish.
Drizzle Thousand Island dressing on top, cover with swiss cheese and top with croutons. Pour 2 tablespoons melted butter over top, dot with pimento and green pepper. bake at 400 for 20 minutes.

name: Most Amazing Reuben Casserole
directions: Layer half the bread cubes in the bottom of a greased 9x13 casserole dish.
Mix the sour cream and onion together.
Layer sauerkraut, crumbled corned beef, sour cream mixture, and cheeses over the bread.
Spread remaining bread cubes over top.
Drizzle with melted butter
Bake 350 for 30 minute.

name: Reuben Bake
directions: Place potato in a saucepan; add water to cover, and bring to a boil.
Cover, reduce heat, and simmer for 20 minutes or until very tender; drain well.
Combine potato, sour cream and the next 3 ingredients in a bowl, and beat at medium speed of a mixer for 2 minutes or until smooth.
Set aside.
Coat a large nonstick skillet with cooking spray, and place over medium heat until hot.
Add the cabbage, corned beef, and caraway seeds, and saute for 4 minutes or until cabbage wilts.
Remove from heat; stir in dressing.
Set aside.
Spread half of potato mixture in the bottom of an 11x7-inch baking dish coated with cooking spray; top with cabbage mixture, and sprinkle with 1 cup cheese.
Spread remaining half of potato mixture over cheese, and top with remaining 1/4 cup cheese.
Sprinkle with paprika.
Bake at 350 for 40 minutes or until golden.
Note:
Can assemble the casserole up to 8 hours ahead of time; cover and chill.  Let stand at room temperature 30 minutes before baking.

name: Reuben Bake
directions: Preheat oven to 350. Coat a 9 x 13 inch baking dish with nonstick spray.
In a large bowl mix together cubed rye bread and the melted butter. Mix well.
Mix together the 1/2 cup sour cream and 1/2 cup Thousand Island dressing; set aside.
Saute onion with 1 tablespoon butter and then mix with the sour cream/Thousand Island dressing mixture.
Layer in pan as follows: 1/2 bread mixture; sauerkraut; corned beef; sour cream and onion mixture; mozzarella cheese; swiss cheese.
Top with rest of bread. Tap down. Bake uncovered for 30 minutes.
